channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Revised Artemis lunar lander plans take shape - SpaceNews
item.description:
1. Revised Artemis lunar lander plans take shape  SpaceNews
  2. What NASA Needs to Stay on Track for the Moon  The New York Times
  3. NASA Marches Toward Artemis III Mission in 2027, Names Crew Members  NASA (.gov)
  4. NASA addresses criticism over all-male crew selected for Artemis III test mission  NBC News
  5. Here Is What NASA's Artemis III Mission Will Do  Jalopnik

item.title: 68 quadrillion underground miles of fungi - The Seattle Times
item.description:
1. 68 quadrillion underground miles of fungi  The Seattle Times
  2. How Plants "Talk" to Each Other Through Underground Root Networks  Yahoo
  3. See the hidden fungal network so big it could stretch to Proxima Centauri and back  Scientific American
  4. Earth's Underground Fungus Network Is So Gigantic That If You Stretched It Out, It Would Reach to Other Star Systems  Futurism
  5. Fungi Expert Is on a Mission to Protect Global Natural Underground Networks  Smithsonian Magazine

item.title: Apollo astronauts trying to sleep on the way to the Moon kept seeing flashes and streaks in the dark, and the cause turned out to be cosmic rays from deep space passing straight through their eyes. - Space Daily
item.description: Apollo astronauts trying to sleep on the way to the Moon kept seeing flashes and streaks in the dark, and the cause turned out to be cosmic rays from deep space passing straight through their eyes.  Space Daily

item.title: Closer every day — A spectacular 30-day collage of the Venus-Jupiter conjunction (photo) - Space
item.description:

  2. This Week's Sky at a Glance, June 12 – 21  Sky & Telescope
  3. Look up: Planetary parade featuring Venus, Jupiter and Mercury set to dazzle the sky tonight  FOX Weather
  4. The Moon Occults Venus  edhat
  5. Don't miss this beautiful 3-planet parade after sunset tonight — it won't last long  Space

item.description:

item.title: Southern California faults hit a 1,000-year stress peak, scientists say the Big One is overdue - Yahoo
item.description:

item.title: The Apollo astronauts who carried lunar dust back into the cabin kept making the same strange report — fresh Moon dust smelled like spent gunpowder — yet the smell never survived the trip home, and more than fifty years later no one has fully explained wh - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The Apollo astronauts who carried lunar dust back into the cabin kept making the same strange report — fresh Moon dust smelled like spent gunpowder — yet the smell never survived the trip home, and more than fifty years later no one has fully explained wh  Space Daily

item.title: Trees may store less planet-heating carbon than hoped, study suggests - The Guardian
item.description:
1. Trees may store less planet-heating carbon than hoped, study suggests  The Guardian

  4. Plants are absorbing more carbon, but not for the reason scientists expected  Earth.com

item.description:

item.title: Beneath Oregon’s Blue Mountains, a single honey fungus has been spreading through the roots of the forest for thousands of years, now covering nearly 10 square kilometres. Mostly hidden underground and betrayed at the surface by dying trees and season - Space Daily
item.description: Beneath Oregon’s Blue Mountains, a single honey fungus has been spreading through the roots of the forest for thousands of years, now covering nearly 10 square kilometres. Mostly hidden underground and betrayed at the surface by dying trees and season  Space Daily
